description: Implement Undo/Redo in Phase 4 Editor using a custom undo stack that tracks changes to translations and approved status, integrates with the Edit menu (Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y), and updates validation/counters on undo/redo.
steps: Create a new module src/stx/gui/undo.py that implements an UndoStack class with: (1) an internal list of UndoCommand objects (each storing row index, column, old_value, new_value), (2) push(command) method, (3) undo() method that restores old_value and returns the command, (4) redo() method that re-applies new_value, (5) can_undo/can_redo properties, (6) clear() method, (7) a Qt Signal 'stack_changed' emitted whenever push/undo/redo/clear is called so the UI can enable/disable buttons.

Modify src/stx/gui/pages/phase4_review.py _EntriesModel.setData() to push an UndoCommand onto the undo stack BEFORE applying the change. The command stores: row, column (TRANSLATION_COL or APPROVED_COL), old_value, new_value. The setData method should apply the new value as it does now.

Add undo() and redo() methods to _EntriesModel that pop/redo from the stack and call setData-like logic internally (without pushing a new command). After applying, emit dataChanged and the edited signal so counters update.
In Phase4ReviewPage._build(), create the UndoStack instance (self._undo_stack). Wire its stack_changed signal to enable/disable undo/redo toolbar buttons.

Add Undo and Redo buttons to the Phase4ReviewPage toolbar (between the status pill area and the 'Load reviewed Excel...' button). Wire them to _undo_stack.undo()/_undo_stack.redo(). Disable when can_undo/can_redo is False.

In src/stx/gui/main_window.py _build_menu(), add 'Undo' (Ctrl+Z) and 'Redo' (Ctrl+Y) actions to the Edit menu (before the Settings entry). These actions should delegate to the Phase4ReviewPage's undo/redo methods when Phase 4 is active. Use QAction.setEnabled() driven by the stack_changed signal.
Ensure that after undo/redo, _update_counters() and _run_auto_validation() are called to refresh the status display.
Clear the undo stack when a new document is loaded (in on_enter() when the model is reset or newly created).

Add unit tests in tests/gui/test_undo.py: (1) test UndoStack push/undo/redo/clear logic standalone, (2) test can_undo/can_redo state transitions, (3) test that undo after multiple pushes restores in reverse order, (4) test that pushing after undo clears the redo stack, (5) test integration with _EntriesModel.setData pushing commands and undo restoring them.
acceptance_criteria: UndoStack class exists in src/stx/gui/undo.py with push/undo/redo/clear/can_undo/can_redo
Editing a translation in Phase4 table pushes a command; Ctrl+Z restores the old value
Toggling approved checkbox pushes a command; Ctrl+Z restores the previous state
Undo/Redo buttons in toolbar are disabled when stack is empty/at end
Edit menu has Undo (Ctrl+Z) and Redo (Ctrl+Y) entries that work when Phase 4 is active
Counters and validation status update after undo/redo
Undo stack is cleared on document reload
All new tests in tests/gui/test_undo.py pass
verification: QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen pytest -q (all tests pass including existing ones)
findings: UndoStack is a QObject (not QWidget) so qtbot.addWidget() cannot be used for it in tests - just instantiate directly. The _applying_undo guard flag prevents infinite recursion when undo/redo calls setData internally. The type annotation uses string literal for forward reference to avoid import issues with UndoStack | None on Python 3.9.
